frame_label: Dress Code: Traditional = Respectable, Other = Questionable
generated_comment: You know, I've always noticed that at family functions, women really shine when they embrace our traditional wear. A beautiful saree or a nice salwar suit just carries so much more dignity and grace. It’s not just about looking good, it's also about how you're perceived, especially by the older generation. It really makes a positive difference.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment subtly implies that traditional Indian attire confers more 'dignity' and 'grace' and leads to better perception, particularly from elders, thereby linking a woman's clothing choice to her respectability and implicitly devaluing other forms of dress. This encodes the 'complimenting a woman for looking 'more decent' in traditional wear' surface manifestation and uses 'elegance/decency framing'.
original_frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 41. Married. A man in Bengaluru. You are posting under your real name on a parenting Facebook group after a thread was started by a childless woman by choice who wrote about feeling judged at family gatherings and asked for advice on how to handle relatives who keep asking when she will have children. You are writing a comment responding to her post and giving your opinion on her situation and whether the relatives asking her are actually wrong and what you think her life is missing that she cannot see yet.
